title: Persist and surface source='model_bridge' through the full paper trade stack
objective: Add a source field to the Trade dataclass, trades DB schema, paper_exec open_position(), and dashboard query so that model-driven paper trades carry source='model_bridge' end-to-end. Paper mode only. No real money. No architecture redesign.
paper_only_notice: THIS IS PAPER MODE ONLY. No real money is involved. No live order placement. No connection to any exchange, broker, or betting platform.
why_this_exists: PAPER_BRIDGE_001 was BLOCKED because source='model_bridge' cannot be persisted without modifying paper_exec.py and db.py, which were listed as do_not_touch. This task expands scope only as far as the persistence path requires. The gate logic from PAPER_BRIDGE_001 is preserved unchanged.

persistence_path.step_1_types: Add source: str = 'bot' to the Trade dataclass in core/types.py. Default 'bot' preserves all existing non-model trades with no behavior change.
persistence_path.step_2_db: Add source column to trades table in core/db.py. Two changes required: (a) schema migration — run ALTER TABLE trades ADD COLUMN source TEXT DEFAULT 'bot' if column does not exist; do NOT rely on CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS to add it to existing databases. (b) update insert_open_trade() to include source in the INSERT. (c) update _row_to_trade() and any SELECT queries in fetch_open_trades() / fetch_recent_closed() to include source column.
persistence_path.step_3_paper_exec: Add source: str = 'bot' parameter to open_position() in core/paper_exec.py. Pass it through to the Trade constructor. Default 'bot' so all existing callers continue to work unchanged.
persistence_path.step_4_dashboard_server: In dashboard_server.py, the _fetch_trades() function currently hard-codes source='bot' for all DB rows. Change it to SELECT source FROM trades and use the actual column value. Existing 'bot' trades will still return source='bot' from the column default. model_bridge trades will return source='model_bridge'.
persistence_path.step_5_model_bridge: core/model_bridge.py implements the gate logic from PAPER_BRIDGE_001 (see preserved_gates below). When calling paper_exec.open_position(), pass source='model_bridge'.
persistence_path.step_6_bot_core: bot_core.py gets a minimal additive call to model_bridge.get_approved_intents() after the existing loop body, as specified in PAPER_BRIDGE_001.

schema_migration_rules: The migration must be additive and backward-safe.
Use ALTER TABLE trades ADD COLUMN source TEXT DEFAULT 'bot' wrapped in a try/except for OperationalError in case the column already exists.
Run this migration inside init_db() or a dedicated migrate_db() call that runs at startup.
Existing rows get source='bot' by default — no data loss, no existing trade behavior change.
Non-model paper trades opened after this patch will get source='bot' by default — no caller changes required except in model_bridge.
preserved_gates: ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE = False at top of model_bridge.py — default False, do not flip to True in submitted patch
Deduplicate shadow log: latest entry per market_slug only, NO_TRADE entries discarded before gate evaluation
action must be BUY_YES or BUY_NO
market type moneyline only — reject slugs containing spread/total/nrfi/props
event date in slug matches today
no existing open position for this slug in DB
one intent per slug per loop
backward_safety_rules: All existing callers of open_position() must continue to work with no changes — the source parameter must default to 'bot'.
All existing trades in the DB must continue to display correctly — the source column default 'bot' covers this.
The Trade dataclass source field must default to 'bot' so existing construction sites do not break.
dashboard_server.py source field change must not break existing bot or manual trade display.
deliverables: core/types.py — source field added to Trade dataclass
core/db.py — source column migration, INSERT update, SELECT update, _row_to_trade update
core/paper_exec.py — source param added to open_position(), passed to Trade
dashboard_server.py — _fetch_trades() reads source from DB column instead of hard-coding 'bot'
core/model_bridge.py — gate logic from PAPER_BRIDGE_001 plus source='model_bridge' in open_position() call
summary of all schema changes made
expected log output: BRIDGE DISABLED (kill switch), BRIDGE GATE PASS, BRIDGE GATE REJECT
acceptance_criteria: Trade dataclass has source field, defaults to 'bot'
trades table has source column, existing rows default to 'bot'
fetch_open_trades() and fetch_recent_closed() return source field
open_position() accepts source param, defaults to 'bot', all existing callers unaffected
dashboard_server._fetch_trades() reads source from DB column, not hard-coded
model_bridge.py passes source='model_bridge' when calling open_position()
kill switch returns empty list and logs BRIDGE DISABLED
log dedup: latest-per-slug only, NO_TRADE discarded before gating
all 13 gates from preserved_gates are implemented
existing non-model paper trades continue to work and display correctly
no changes to risk.py, signal_base.py, dashboard.html
no real execution path exists — paper only
verification: Confirm logs show: BRIDGE DISABLED — set ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=True to activate
Confirm existing open positions still appear correctly in runtime/state.json
Check /api/trades payload: existing trades show source='bot', no regressions
Manually flip ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=True in model_bridge.py, rerun, check for BRIDGE GATE PASS or BRIDGE GATE REJECT log lines
If a gate pass occurs, confirm new position in state.json has source='model_bridge'
Flip ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE back to False before writing result
rollback: Revert core/types.py, core/db.py, core/paper_exec.py, dashboard_server.py, bot_core.py. Delete core/model_bridge.py. The source column in the DB will remain but is harmless — existing data is unaffected.
